cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The passage is about flea markets and garage sales in the U.S. The question specifically asks about garage sales. First, I need to recall what the passage says about garage sales. Let me read the relevant part again. It says, "Perhaps even more enjoyable than flea markets are the weekend outdoor garage sales which have now become very much a part of American culture. These are held usually in the spring and fall when the weather is still good." So, garage sales are part of American culture and are held in spring and fall. The options are A to D. Let's check each one. Option A: It is only held in the spring of the year. The passage says "usually in the spring and fall," so it's not only spring. So A is incorrect. Option B: The goods on display are totally useless. The passage says that the homeowner is clearing out accumulated items that are no longer useful to him. But the items are in good condition and can be great buys. So "totally useless" is opposite to what's stated. B is wrong. Option C: Garage sales are popular with Americans. The passage says they've become very much part of American culture and are held on weekends. That implies popularity. So C seems correct.
